title: Workflow agent dispatch
description: One per agent() spawned by the Workflow tool. Workflow-runtime agents do NOT fire Claude Code's Agent/Task PreToolUse or SubagentStart hooks, so they are invisible to the live bus per-agent. This channel is reconstructed by the reflexarc workflow_dispatch ingester reading the on-disk workflow journal + agent transcripts (complete across all phases, never pruned), giving full per-agent dispatch visibility — the prompt the orchestrator handed each workflow agent, its label, and lineage back to the parent session.

ts.description: RFC3339 UTC dispatch timestamp (agent transcript line-0 timestamp).

agent_id.description: Stable per-agent hex id (equals the transcript agentId).

slug.description: Per-RUN human slug shared across the run's agents (e.g. 'buzzing-leaping-bentley'). NOT a per-agent label — the agent() label lives only in the workflow JS source, not on disk; the prompt is the authoritative per-agent signal.

model.description: Model the agent ran under, when resolvable from the transcript.

parent_session_id.description: The orchestrator session that launched the workflow (transcript sessionId).

project.description: Project slug derived from the agent cwd.

cwd.description: Working directory the agent ran in.

prompt.description: The dispatch prompt handed to the agent, bounded for bus transport. The full untruncated prompt is at transcript_path line 0.

prompt_chars.description: Full (untruncated) prompt length in characters — so consumers know how much the bound clipped.

transcript_path.description: Absolute path to the agent transcript whose line 0 holds the full prompt (the join target for deep analysis).
